user_intent: There are 3 boxes, all 3 boxes are labeled incorrectly. the Three labels are apples, oranges and apples, oranges. You can take one fruit out of one box to determine what label is supposed to go on each box. Which box do you take a fruit out of.

final_storm.prompt:
CONTEXT:


USER: There are 3 boxes, all 3 boxes are labeled incorrectly. the Three labels are apples, oranges and apples, oranges. You can take one fruit out of one box to determine what label is supposed to go on each box. Which box do you take a fruit out of.

trajectories.text:
This is a classic riddle! Here’s the solution:

You take out a fruit from **the box labeled "apples and oranges"**.

Here’s why:

If it’s the box labeled “apples and oranges,” then the box labeled “apples” *must* contain the apples, and the box labeled “oranges” *must* contain the oranges.  Taking out a fruit from the “apples” box will immediately tell you which label is the correct one.

If you take out an apple, you immediately know that box contains the apples label. Taking out an orange from that box will tell you that box contains the oranges label.
